extra_fields.detailed_description: Research and identify potential grant opportunities, investors, and other funding sources relevant to the Clear English project.
Develop compelling grant proposals and applications tailored to specific funding opportunities, highlighting the project's goals, impact, and sustainability.
Establish communication and build relationships with potential funders, presenting the Clear English project and addressing their questions and concerns.
Negotiate the terms and conditions of funding agreements with successful applicants, ensuring alignment with the project's goals and objectives.

task_children.description: Establish Editorial Board and Linguistic Review Process

extra_fields.detailed_description: Identify and research potential candidates for the editorial board, considering expertise, experience, and diversity.
Establish clear and objective criteria for selecting editorial board members, including expertise in linguistics, education, and technical writing.
Create detailed documentation outlining the linguistic review process, including submission guidelines, review criteria, and decision-making protocols.
Define clear communication channels and protocols for the editorial board and linguistic review process, ensuring efficient and transparent communication.

Identify all individuals, groups, or organizations that have an interest in the project or may be affected by its outcome. This includes internal teams, external partners, regulatory bodies, and potential users of Clear English. Create a stakeholder register with contact information, roles, and levels of influence.

Clearly articulate the overall goals of the Clear English project and break them down into specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) objectives. This includes defining the scope of linguistic changes, target audience, and desired outcomes. Document these goals and objectives in a project charter.

Identify the tangible outputs of the project, such as the Clear English standard, reference dictionary, style guide, pilot curriculum, and public licensing policy. Define the specifications and acceptance criteria for each deliverable. Create a deliverables register.

Define the metrics that will be used to measure the success of the project, such as comprehension speed, ordinal error rate, pronunciation consistency, and learner retention. Establish baseline measurements and target values for each metric. Document the success criteria in the project charter.

Identify any assumptions that are being made about the project scope, such as the availability of resources, the cooperation of stakeholders, or the stability of the linguistic landscape. Document any constraints that may limit the project scope, such as budget limitations, time constraints, or regulatory requirements. Create a scope management plan.
Establish the methodology for managing the project's scope, including how changes will be handled and controlled.
Develop a detailed project schedule outlining all tasks, dependencies, and timelines using project management software.
Assign specific resources (personnel, equipment, budget) to each task in the project schedule.
Define how project information will be communicated to stakeholders, including frequency, channels, and responsible parties.
Create documentation outlining all project management processes, including scope management, schedule management, resource allocation, and communication.

Determine all individuals, groups, or organizations that have an interest in or may be affected by the Clear English project. This includes linguists, educators, technical writers, ESL publishers, standards organizations, and potential users.

Evaluate the level of influence and interest each stakeholder has in the project. This will help prioritize engagement efforts and tailor communication strategies to address their specific needs and concerns.

Create a detailed plan outlining how to effectively engage with each stakeholder group throughout the project lifecycle. This includes defining communication channels, frequency of updates, and methods for gathering feedback.

Gather and document the specific requirements, expectations, and concerns of each stakeholder group. This information will be used to inform project decisions and ensure that the Clear English standard meets the needs of its intended users.

Identify all potential risks that could impact the project, including technical, financial, and operational risks. This involves brainstorming sessions, reviewing project documentation, and consulting with subject matter experts.

Evaluate the likelihood of each identified risk occurring and the potential impact on the project's timeline, budget, and deliverables. This involves using qualitative and quantitative risk assessment techniques.

Rank the identified risks based on their probability and impact scores to determine which risks require the most attention and mitigation efforts. This involves creating a risk matrix and using a risk scoring system.

Develop specific mitigation strategies for each high-priority risk, outlining actions to reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk. This involves brainstorming potential solutions, evaluating their feasibility, and documenting the chosen strategies.

Document the entire risk assessment process, including the identified risks, their probability and impact scores, prioritization, and mitigation strategies. This involves creating a risk register and maintaining it throughout the project lifecycle.

task_children.description: Linguistic Rule Design and Corpus Creation

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze how ordinals are currently used in various contexts (written, spoken, formal, informal) to identify inconsistencies and patterns.
Based on the research, create several potential rule sets for standardizing ordinals in Clear English, considering simplicity, consistency, and intelligibility.
Assess the clarity and ease of understanding of each rule set through cognitive testing and expert review, focusing on potential ambiguities and edge cases.
Incorporate feedback from the evaluation phase to refine the most promising rule set, ensuring it aligns with the overall goals of Clear English and is readily understandable.

Objective: Thoroughly investigate established phonetic systems used for English. Scope: Review IPA, ARPABET, and other relevant systems. Steps: Literature review, online research, expert consultation. Deliverables: Summary report of findings.

Objective: Identify and categorize common inconsistencies in English spelling-to-sound correspondences. Scope: Focus on frequently occurring exceptions and ambiguous cases. Steps: Corpus analysis, rule extraction, exception listing. Deliverables: Database of inconsistencies.

Objective: Create a set of consistent and unambiguous spelling-to-sound rules for Clear English. Scope: Cover all common English phonemes and graphemes. Steps: Rule design, testing, refinement. Deliverables: Rule set document.

Objective: Evaluate the effectiveness of the Clear English phonetic rules using a text-to-speech engine. Scope: Assess pronunciation accuracy and naturalness. Steps: Rule implementation, text input, audio output analysis. Deliverables: Evaluation report.

task_children.description: Refine rules based on testing feedback

extra_fields.detailed_description: Objective: Improve the Clear English phonetic rules based on the results of text-to-speech testing and expert feedback. Scope: Address identified errors and inconsistencies. Steps: Rule revision, re-testing, validation. Deliverables: Final rule set.

Objective: Identify common irregular verbs and nouns in English.
Scope: Focus on the most frequently used irregular forms.
Steps: Analyze a corpus of English text, consult linguistic resources, and create a list of target words.
Deliverables: A comprehensive list of irregular verbs and nouns.

Objective: Create rules for regularizing irregular verbs and nouns.
Scope: Ensure rules are consistent and easy to apply.
Steps: Define rules for verb conjugation and noun pluralization, test rules on sample texts, and refine rules based on feedback.
Deliverables: A set of clear and consistent regularization rules.

Objective: Evaluate the impact of regularization on language comprehension.
Scope: Measure comprehension levels of regularized vs. irregular forms.
Steps: Conduct comprehension tests with native and non-native speakers, analyze test results, and identify potential issues.
Deliverables: A report on the impact of regularization on intelligibility.

Objective: Improve regularization rules based on intelligibility testing.
Scope: Address issues identified in the intelligibility assessment.
Steps: Revise rules based on test results, conduct further testing, and finalize the regularization rules.
Deliverables: Finalized set of morphological regularization rules.
Identify common irregular verb conjugations and noun pluralizations to target for regularization.
Create rules for regularizing identified irregular forms, ensuring consistency and intelligibility.
Evaluate the impact of regularization rules on the comprehension and naturalness of the language.
Adjust regularization rules based on feedback from linguists, educators, and usability testers.
Identify diverse and representative text sources, including books, articles, websites, and transcripts, ensuring a balanced representation of different genres and styles.
Obtain necessary permissions for using copyrighted materials, download or transcribe text data, and clean the data by removing irrelevant content, correcting errors, and standardizing formatting.

Tokenize, tag, and parse the text data using natural language processing tools, and annotate the corpus with relevant linguistic information, such as part-of-speech tags, syntactic structures, and semantic roles.
Store the processed and annotated corpus data in a database or file system, and implement a data management system to ensure data integrity, accessibility, and security.
Determine the scope of the dictionary (e.g., core vocabulary, specialized terms) and define its structure (e.g., entry format, part-of-speech tagging).
Collect word data from the reference corpus and other sources, analyze word frequencies and usage patterns, and identify potential inconsistencies and ambiguities.
Write dictionary entries for each word, including definitions, pronunciations, example sentences, and usage notes, following the defined dictionary structure and style guidelines.
Review and edit dictionary entries for accuracy, clarity, consistency, and adherence to the Clear English rules and style guidelines.
Implement the dictionary in a digital format (e.g., online database, mobile app) that is accessible and user-friendly, and integrate it with other Clear English resources.

task_children.description: Analyze Ordinal Rule Impact on Corpus

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the impact of proposed ordinal rules on the reference corpus to ensure consistency and identify potential conflicts.
Analyze the impact of proposed spelling-to-sound rules on the reference dictionary to ensure pronunciation consistency.

task_children.description: Analyze Morphology Rule Impact on Corpus

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the impact of proposed morphology rules on the reference corpus to ensure grammatical correctness and identify potential ambiguities.

task_children.description: Analyze Homograph Rule Impact on Corpus

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the impact of proposed homograph rules on the reference corpus to ensure semantic clarity and identify potential misinterpretations.

task_children.description: Editorial Board Review of Linguistic Scope

extra_fields.detailed_description: The editorial board reviews the overall linguistic scope of Clear English, considering the impact of all rule sets on the corpus and dictionary.
Define the specific learning outcomes for each module, aligning with Clear English principles and target audience needs.
Develop a logical and engaging module structure, incorporating Clear English rules and examples, and creating interactive exercises.
Create a variety of exercises and activities to reinforce learning, including quizzes, games, and real-world application scenarios.
Conduct thorough reviews of all learning materials, incorporating feedback from linguists, educators, and target audience representatives.

Clearly define the learning outcomes and skills that the assessments will measure. Establish specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) criteria for evaluating student performance.

Develop a variety of assessment instruments, such as quizzes, tests, writing prompts, and oral presentations, that align with the defined objectives and criteria. Ensure that the instruments are appropriate for the target audience and learning context.

Conduct pilot testing of the assessment instruments with a small group of learners to identify any issues with clarity, validity, or reliability. Gather feedback from learners and educators to refine the instruments.

Develop clear and consistent scoring rubrics and guidelines for evaluating student performance on the assessment instruments. Ensure that the rubrics are aligned with the assessment objectives and criteria.

Implement measures to ensure the validity and reliability of the assessments, such as using multiple assessment methods, conducting statistical analysis, and seeking expert review. Address any issues identified during pilot testing or analysis.
Establish clear criteria for selecting participants, including demographics, language proficiency, and learning goals. This ensures a representative and suitable cohort for testing.
Research and identify potential participants through ESL programs, community centers, and online platforms. Contact them with information about the pilot program and recruitment process.
Screen potential participants based on the defined criteria. Enroll eligible participants and obtain informed consent for participation in the pilot program.
Coordinate with participants to schedule pilot sessions at convenient times and locations. Ensure adequate resources and facilities are available for each session.
Define the scope and objectives of the usability testing, identify target users, and develop a detailed test plan.
Set up the testing environment, prepare testing materials (e.g., prototypes, scenarios, tasks), and ensure that all necessary equipment is available and functioning properly.
Facilitate usability testing sessions with participants, observe their interactions with the learning materials, and collect data on their performance and feedback.
Analyze the data collected during the usability testing sessions, identify usability issues and areas for improvement, and prepare a report summarizing the findings.

Objective: Ensure data quality for accurate analysis.
Scope: Data cleaning, validation, and formatting.
Steps: Remove duplicates, correct errors, standardize formats.
Deliverables: Cleaned and prepared dataset.

Objective: Summarize key characteristics of the pilot data.
Scope: Calculate descriptive statistics (mean, median, standard deviation).
Steps: Use statistical software to generate descriptive statistics for relevant variables.
Deliverables: Summary report of descriptive statistics.

Objective: Compare outcomes between control and experimental groups.
Scope: Statistical tests to determine significant differences.
Steps: Select appropriate statistical tests (t-tests, ANOVA), perform tests, interpret results.
Deliverables: Report on comparative analysis findings.

Objective: Understand the implications of the data analysis.
Scope: Interpret statistical findings in the context of Clear English.
Steps: Review analysis reports, discuss findings with stakeholders, formulate conclusions.
Deliverables: Interpretation of results and conclusions report.

Objective: Clean, format, and prepare pilot data for statistical analysis.
Scope: Includes data from all pilot cohorts on comprehension, pronunciation, and learner retention.
Steps: Data cleaning, outlier removal, formatting for statistical software.
Deliverables: Cleaned and formatted data files.

Objective: Calculate descriptive statistics (mean, median, standard deviation) for key metrics.
Scope: Includes comprehension speed, ordinal error rate, pronunciation consistency, and learner retention.
Steps: Calculate descriptive statistics for each metric, create summary tables.
Deliverables: Summary tables of descriptive statistics.

Objective: Conduct inferential statistical tests (t-tests, ANOVA) to compare results across groups.
Scope: Includes comparing performance of different cohorts or learning materials.
Steps: Select appropriate statistical tests, conduct tests, interpret results.
Deliverables: Statistical test results and interpretations.

Objective: Create visualizations (graphs, charts) to present key findings.
Scope: Includes visualizing trends in comprehension, pronunciation, and learner retention.
Steps: Create visualizations, write a report summarizing the findings.
Deliverables: Visualizations and a summary report.

Objective: Identify suitable open-source licenses for Clear English.
Scope: Review licenses like MIT, Apache 2.0, GPL, Creative Commons.
Steps: 1. Research common open-source licenses. 2. Analyze their terms and conditions. 3. Assess their compatibility with Clear English goals.
Deliverables: A report summarizing suitable open-source licenses.

Objective: Establish the specific terms and conditions for using Clear English.
Scope: Define usage rights, restrictions, attribution requirements, and commercial use policies.
Steps: 1. Determine the desired level of openness. 2. Draft licensing terms based on research. 3. Review terms with legal counsel.
Deliverables: A draft of the licensing terms and conditions.

Objective: Gather feedback from stakeholders on the proposed licensing policy.
Scope: Solicit input from educators, developers, and the general public.
Steps: 1. Prepare a summary of the proposed licensing policy. 2. Conduct surveys and focus groups. 3. Analyze feedback and identify areas for improvement.
Deliverables: A report summarizing stakeholder feedback.

task_children.description: Draft the public licensing policy document

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Create a comprehensive and legally sound licensing policy document.
Scope: Incorporate feedback from stakeholders and legal counsel.
Steps: 1. Revise the draft licensing terms based on feedback. 2. Format the document for public release. 3. Obtain final approval from legal counsel.
Deliverables: A final public licensing policy document.

Define the sections, chapters, and appendices needed for the Clear English standard documentation. This includes determining the level of detail required for each section and ensuring a logical flow of information.
Write the section detailing the rules for ordinals, spelling-to-sound, morphology, and homographs. Ensure clarity, consistency, and accuracy in the presentation of these rules.
Develop examples and illustrations to demonstrate the application of Clear English rules. These examples should be relevant, easy to understand, and representative of real-world usage.
Establish a style guide for the Clear English documentation, including formatting guidelines, typography, and visual elements. This ensures a consistent and professional presentation.
Thoroughly review and edit the documentation for accuracy, clarity, and consistency. This includes proofreading for errors, verifying examples, and ensuring adherence to the style guide.
Ensure the website, servers, and any necessary software are fully operational and tested for the Clear English standard launch.
Craft press releases, social media posts, and website content to announce the Clear English standard to the public.
Organize and execute a launch event to generate excitement and awareness for the Clear English standard.

task_children.description: Monitor Launch Performance and User Feedback

extra_fields.detailed_description: Track website traffic, social media engagement, and user feedback to assess the initial impact of the Clear English standard launch.
Define specific demographics and professional groups to target with Clear English promotion. Research and select appropriate communication channels (e.g., social media, conferences, publications).
Create compelling content showcasing the benefits of Clear English, including website content, brochures, presentations, and social media posts. Tailor materials to different target audiences.

Implement the marketing plan across selected channels, including social media advertising, email marketing, conference presentations, and partnerships with relevant organizations. Track campaign performance and adjust strategies as needed.

task_children.description: Engage with Key Influencers and Stakeholders

extra_fields.detailed_description: Identify and engage with influential figures in linguistics, education, and technical writing to promote Clear English and build credibility. Seek endorsements and partnerships.

Track key metrics such as website traffic, social media engagement, and adoption rates to assess the effectiveness of promotion efforts. Gather feedback from users and stakeholders to inform future strategies.

Establish specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) metrics for adoption across different user groups (e.g., ESL learners, technical writers). Set realistic targets for adoption rates within the project timeline.

task_children.description: Identify Key User Groups and Pathways

extra_fields.detailed_description: Segment potential users into distinct groups (e.g., ESL programs, technical writing teams, K-12 schools). Map out the specific steps and resources required for each group to adopt Clear English.

task_children.description: Engage Early Adopters and Gather Feedback

extra_fields.detailed_description: Recruit a small group of early adopters from each user group to pilot Clear English. Collect feedback on their experiences, including ease of use, perceived benefits, and any challenges encountered.

task_children.description: Analyze Adoption Data and Refine Strategy

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the data collected from early adopters to identify areas for improvement in the adoption pathway. Refine the strategy based on the feedback and insights gained.

task_children.description: Analyze current funding sources and stability

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Assess the reliability and sustainability of existing funding streams.
Scope: Review grant agreements, licensing contracts, and other financial documents.
Steps: 1. Identify all current funding sources. 2. Evaluate the terms and conditions of each funding agreement. 3. Assess the financial stability of each funding source. 4. Document findings in a report.
Resources Needed: ['Funding and Licensing Manager', 'Financial Advisor']

Objective: Identify potential new funding sources to diversify the project's financial base.
Scope: Research grant opportunities, potential investors, and other funding avenues.
Steps: 1. Research relevant grant databases and funding organizations. 2. Identify potential investors or sponsors. 3. Evaluate the eligibility criteria and application requirements for each opportunity. 4. Document findings in a report.
Resources Needed: ['Funding and Licensing Manager', 'Project Manager']

task_children.description: Refine business plan and value proposition

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Strengthen the project's business plan and clearly articulate the value proposition of Clear English to potential funders.
Scope: Review and update the business plan, focusing on financial projections and the benefits of Clear English.
Steps: 1. Review the existing business plan. 2. Update financial projections based on current data and market trends. 3. Refine the value proposition to highlight the benefits of Clear English for different stakeholders. 4. Document changes in the business plan.
Resources Needed: ['Project Manager', 'Marketing Strategist', 'Financial Advisor']

Objective: Build relationships with potential funding partners and secure commitments for future funding.
Scope: Conduct outreach to grant organizations, investors, and other potential funders.
Steps: 1. Identify key contacts at target funding organizations. 2. Prepare presentation materials highlighting the project's goals and impact. 3. Schedule meetings with potential funders. 4. Follow up with funders to answer questions and address concerns.
Resources Needed: ['Community Engagement Coordinator', 'Funding and Licensing Manager', 'Project Manager']

Establish clear criteria for selecting members of the governance board, including expertise, stakeholder representation, and commitment to the project's goals. This involves researching best practices for board composition and developing a transparent selection process.

Develop a comprehensive governance charter outlining the board's roles, responsibilities, decision-making processes, and conflict resolution mechanisms. This includes drafting bylaws that define the board's structure, membership, and operating procedures.

Define clear communication protocols for the governance board, including meeting schedules, reporting requirements, and channels for disseminating information to stakeholders. This involves developing a communication plan that ensures transparency and accountability.

Present the proposed governance structure to key stakeholders for review and approval. This involves conducting presentations, soliciting feedback, and incorporating suggestions to ensure buy-in and support for the governance framework.
Set up various channels for collecting user feedback, including online forms, email, and social media monitoring.
Implement tools and processes to track the usage of Clear English across different platforms and user groups.

task_children.description: Analyze User Feedback and Usage Data

extra_fields.detailed_description: Regularly analyze user feedback and usage data to identify areas for improvement and potential issues.

task_children.description: Report on Usage and Feedback Trends

extra_fields.detailed_description: Prepare regular reports summarizing usage trends and user feedback for stakeholders.

task_children.description: Gather User Feedback and Usage Data

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collect user feedback from various channels (forums, surveys, support tickets) and analyze usage data to identify areas for improvement in the Clear English standard.
Categorize and prioritize collected feedback based on frequency, impact, and feasibility of implementation. Identify recurring themes and critical issues.
Evaluate the potential impact of proposed changes on intelligibility, learnability, and consistency of the Clear English standard. Consider potential trade-offs.
Implement approved updates to the Clear English standard, documentation, and learning materials. Conduct thorough testing to ensure quality and consistency.
Communicate updates to the Clear English standard to stakeholders, including users, educators, and developers. Provide clear documentation and support for adopting the changes.

Establish a system to accurately track all licensing agreements, associated revenue, and payment schedules. This includes setting up databases, spreadsheets, or specialized software to manage the data.

Lead negotiations with potential licensees to establish mutually beneficial licensing terms, including royalty rates, usage rights, and duration of the agreement. Ensure compliance with legal and ethical standards.

Monitor licensee compliance with the terms of the licensing agreements, including usage rights, payment schedules, and quality control measures. Take appropriate action to address any violations or breaches of contract.

Establish a process for receiving and processing royalty payments from licensees, generating accurate royalty reports, and distributing payments to relevant stakeholders. Ensure compliance with tax regulations and accounting standards.

Objective: Ensure the governance charter and decision-making processes are clearly defined and effectively implemented.
Scope: Review the existing governance charter, decision-making workflows, and communication protocols.
Steps: 1. Gather all relevant governance documents. 2. Analyze the clarity and completeness of the charter. 3. Assess the efficiency of decision-making processes. 4. Identify any gaps or areas for improvement.
Deliverables: A report summarizing the review findings and recommendations for improvement.

Objective: Evaluate the performance of the editorial board in maintaining consistent editorial standards and resolving linguistic disputes.
Scope: Assess the board's effectiveness in reviewing and approving linguistic rules, resolving conflicts, and ensuring consistency across the Clear English standard.
Steps: 1. Review meeting minutes and decision logs. 2. Analyze the consistency of editorial decisions. 3. Gather feedback from board members and stakeholders. 4. Identify any areas where the board's performance can be improved.
Deliverables: A performance assessment report with recommendations for enhancing the board's effectiveness.

Objective: Gather and analyze user feedback on the governance structure and editorial control mechanisms.
Scope: Collect feedback from stakeholders regarding their satisfaction with the governance structure, their perception of fairness and transparency, and their suggestions for improvement.
Steps: 1. Design and distribute a survey to stakeholders. 2. Conduct focus groups with representative user groups. 3. Analyze the survey and focus group data to identify key themes and concerns. 4. Prepare a report summarizing the feedback and recommendations.
Deliverables: A user feedback report with actionable insights for improving governance and editorial control.

Objective: Ensure that the editorial standards are consistently applied across all aspects of the Clear English standard.
Scope: Review the application of editorial standards in the reference dictionary, learning materials, and documentation.
Steps: 1. Select a sample of entries from the reference dictionary. 2. Review a selection of learning materials. 3. Examine the standard documentation. 4. Assess whether the editorial standards have been consistently applied.
Deliverables: A report on the consistency of editorial standards with recommendations.
